meaning: n.必胜信念者,必胜主义者
examples: She exerted an iron hold on the culture, preferring Beethoven's Sixth Symphony to his triumphalist Fifth, disliking Respighi's "Pines of Rome".
America's policy towards Russia was not triumphalist.
She warned the Chinese media, which have issued triumphalist reports, "please don't fabricate stories."
It may, but the triumphalist mood in Addis Ababa gives small sign that it will.
As he briefed the media about today's agreement, Alex Salmond confided that he had been told by his advisers: do not look triumphalist.
